short_description: A formally designated focal point in City Hall serving as the single entry point for refugee-related matters, referrals and complex cases.
long_description: Designate a municipal Refugee Focal Point who understands the legal framework, rights and procedures applicable to refugees and Temporary Protection holders; maintains up-to-date information on needs; makes referrals across departments and national institutions; supports complex cases; and anchors municipal leadership in the Local Refugee Coordination Forum.
target_group: Refugees, TP holders and municipal departments
scale_up_potential: High — replicable in any municipality at low cost
source_lap: Comrat Local Action Plan for Refugee Inclusion (2026)

short_description: Home-based care, facility upgrades and a trained buddy network for older residents, persons with disabilities and isolated households.
long_description: Upgrade local care by linking facility improvements with home-based support and trained volunteers. Social workers, nurses and community buddies provide home visits, well-being checks, medication support, food access and companionship. Responds to Comrat's ageing caseload: 27.9% of surveyed members are aged 60+ and 45.6% of households report a chronic condition.
target_group: Older persons, persons with disabilities, single caregivers
scale_up_potential: High — model transferable to other ageing districts
source_lap: Comrat Local Action Plan for Refugee Inclusion (2026)

short_description: Service housing to attract and retain medical staff, paired with a telemedicine point connecting patients to specialists.
long_description: Strengthen local healthcare capacity by attracting doctors, nurses and specialists — including qualified refugee professionals where legally possible — through service housing and recruitment support, while equipping a telemedicine point in the polyclinic to connect complex cases to specialists in larger cities and reduce unnecessary travel.
target_group: All residents; patients with complex or chronic conditions
implementation_owner: Comrat City Hall + district health authorities
scale_up_potential: Medium — depends on national health workforce policy
source_lap: Comrat Local Action Plan for Refugee Inclusion (2026)

short_description: Scholarships and stipends for refugee and vulnerable local students, linked to service-learning such as tutoring younger pupils.
long_description: Partner with Comrat State University, colleges and vocational institutions to provide scholarships or stipend top-ups combining academic potential with vulnerability. Recipients commit to service-learning — tutoring, learning-hub support — creating a self-sustaining loop between higher education and community learning support.
target_group: Refugee and vulnerable local youth and students
implementation_owner: Comrat City Hall + Comrat State University
scale_up_potential: High — scalable fund model; replicable per city
source_lap: Comrat Local Action Plan for Refugee Inclusion (2026)

short_description: Upgrades to underused public spaces and civic infrastructure serving refugee and host neighbourhoods alike.
long_description: Revitalize underused urban land, public spaces and civic infrastructure — lighting, green space, safe routes, seating, play areas — in neighbourhoods where needs concentrate. Investments upgrade shared spaces used by all residents rather than creating separate refugee facilities, and can incorporate energy-efficient and nature-based solutions.
scale_up_potential: High — bankable as part of regional public-works packages
source_lap: Comrat Local Action Plan for Refugee Inclusion (2026)

short_description: A social taxi and community shuttle providing accessible, low-cost mobility for older persons, persons with disabilities and students.
long_description: Establish a targeted municipal mobility service complementing regular transport: an accessible social-taxi vehicle for on-demand medical and social trips, plus a scheduled community shuttle linking peripheral neighbourhoods to schools, clinics and services. Provides universal mobility for refugees and local residents, with simple eligibility linked to social assistance.
target_group: Older persons, persons with disabilities, students, low-income households
scale_up_potential: Medium–high — operating model replicable across raions
source_lap: Comrat Local Action Plan for Refugee Inclusion (2026)

short_description: A municipal mechanism connecting vulnerable tenants with private landlords: unit registry, lease formalization, mediation and a small guarantee fund.
long_description: Create a Social Rental Agency that recruits property owners, maintains a registry of affordable units, formalizes leases and mediates disputes, with a small guarantee fund covering deposits and minor repairs. Responds to widespread informal tenure (57.5% of surveyed households lack a formal lease) and makes the private rental market safer for tenants and landlords alike.
target_group: Refugee and vulnerable local tenants; private landlords
scale_up_potential: High — core model for inter-municipal housing pipeline
source_lap: Comrat Local Action Plan for Refugee Inclusion (2026)

short_description: A community garden and greenhouse, community kitchen and periodic eco-market combining food security, skills and social cohesion.
long_description: Develop an agri-food and social inclusion hub where refugees and local residents grow produce, prepare meals, support a social canteen function and sell selected products through a periodic eco-market. Links food security, practical skills, local producer visibility and everyday interaction in one package, and can enhance climate resilience along the Yalpug river corridor.
target_group: Vulnerable households; small producers; community members
source_lap: Comrat Local Action Plan for Refugee Inclusion (2026)

short_description: Public-private partnership housing on municipal land, reserving a share of units for vulnerable local and refugee families.
long_description: Develop mixed-income affordable housing through partnership with private developers and development finance: the municipality contributes land or an unused asset and infrastructure connections; private partners finance construction. A share of units is reserved for vulnerable households while other units cross-subsidize operations, bundling energy efficiency and maintenance obligations into the PPP contract.
target_group: Vulnerable local and refugee families; municipal housing stock
scale_up_potential: High — flagship for aggregated IFI investment across cities
source_lap: Comrat Local Action Plan for Refugee Inclusion (2026)

short_description: Short-term paid public works — greening, river corridor cleanup, public-space upkeep — with fair wages and skills certificates.
long_description: Create short-term paid public-works and municipal internship opportunities for refugees and vulnerable locals: park maintenance, tree planting, cleanup of the Yalpug corridor, waste-separation pilots and public-space upkeep. Participants receive fair wages, safety training, certificates and referrals to longer-term employment, while the city gains visible environmental improvements.
target_group: Unemployed refugees and vulnerable local residents
scale_up_potential: High — cohort model easily repeated and scaled
source_lap: Comrat Local Action Plan for Refugee Inclusion (2026)

short_description: Business training, mentoring, micro-grants and childcare-friendly formats helping refugee and local women start or grow micro-businesses.
long_description: Support refugee and local women to start or expand micro-businesses and social enterprises through training, mentoring, legal and start-up advice, micro-grants, buyer days and referrals to national SME instruments. Childcare-friendly formats respond directly to the caregiving barrier — around 25% of respondents cite care responsibilities as their main obstacle to work.
target_group: Refugee and local women entrepreneurs and jobseekers
scale_up_potential: High — hub model prepared for multi-city rollout
source_lap: Comrat Local Action Plan for Refugee Inclusion (2026)

short_description: Practical digital and AI skills workshops — e-services, remote work, AI tools — hosted in the library and community spaces.
long_description: Offer practical workshops helping refugees and residents use digital tools for work, study and services: e-government platforms, digital payments, job platforms, online safety, and AI tools for translation, CV drafting and productivity. Dedicated tracks for women, youth and older persons prepare participants for freelance and higher-skilled work and help diversify the local economy.
scale_up_potential: High — curriculum replicable across all CoS cities
source_lap: Comrat Local Action Plan for Refugee Inclusion (2026)

action_title: Refugee Focal Point in City Hall
short_description: Appoint or formally designate a municipal Refugee Focal Point as the main entry point for refugee-related matters.
long_description: Appoint or formally designate a municipal Refugee Focal Point as the main entry point for refugee-related matters. The focal point should understand the legal framework, rights and procedures applicable to refugees, Temporary Protection holders, asylum-seekers and other persons of concern; maintain updated information on needs; make appropriate referrals; support complex cases across departments and national institutions; and strengthen municipal leadership in local coordination structures, including refugee coordination meetings.
source_lap: Bălți LAP workshop — example action bank

short_description: Gradually expand the Refugee Focal Point into a municipal inclusion and integration center serving refugees, other displaced persons and foreigners more broadly.
long_description: Gradually expand the Refugee Focal Point into a municipal inclusion and integration center serving refugees, other displaced persons and foreigners more broadly. Located in City Hall, a library or community hub, the center would consolidate access to information, counseling and referrals through municipal staff and partner organizations, with interpretation and multilingual materials as needed. Over time, it would also help the municipality attract and integrate foreign workers, students and residents, especially where labour shortages and economic zones create demand for a structured local integration function.
source_lap: Bălți LAP workshop — example action bank

short_description: Facilitate refugee access to national and municipal social assistance, including child allowances, disability-related support, heating subsidies and other locally available benefits.
long_description: Facilitate refugee access to national and municipal social assistance, including child allowances, disability-related support, heating subsidies and other locally available benefits. Municipal social workers, ATAS/STAS and protection partners would provide outreach, multilingual information, application support and accompaniment for complex cases. The action should also document recurring barriers and raise them through municipal-national coordination channels, helping prevent destitution while strengthening the local safety net for vulnerable refugee and host-community households.
source_lap: Bălți LAP workshop — example action bank

action_title: Emergency Aid Fund for Refugee Families

long_description: Establish or expand a small municipal emergency fund for refugee and low-income local households facing acute hardship. The fund should be transparent, time-bound and managed with trusted NGOs, community foundations, faith groups or the Red Cross, using municipal allocations, donor grants and charitable contributions.

long_description: Upgrade local care for older persons, persons with disabilities, single caregivers and isolated residents by linking facility improvements with home-based support and trained volunteers. long_description: Provide free or discounted public transport passes to refugees, students, jobseekers, older persons, persons with disabilities and low-income households who need help accessing schools, healthcare, employment opportunities and integration activities.
